{"body":"Reported in #32203.","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGVAbKA","author":{"login":"balhoff"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent please investigate and propose what to do.","createdAt":"2026-06-16T14:07:24Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[{"content":"EYES","users":{"totalCount":1}}],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32207#issuecomment-4719647528","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGVAtWg","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"🤖 Working on it... Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27623490394) *— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-06-16T14:07:52Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32207#issuecomment-4719652186","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGVIYJA","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"## Investigation ### What changed in Rhea RHEA:10836 has been obsoleted upstream. Confirmed via the live Rhea SPARQL endpoint: > "The reaction has been replaced by RHEA:86143 and RHEA:86147" This is a **split**, not a rename. What was modeled as a single NADPH-dependent monooxygenase is now modeled as two separate enzymatic steps (a bienzyme mechanism described in [Qi et al. 2021, JACS, PMID:34586791](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34586791/) — "Bienzyme-Catalytic and Dioxygenation-Mediated Anthraquinone Ring Opening"): | Old (obsolete) | New replacements | |---|---| | RHEA:10836 `questin + NADPH + O2 = demethylsulochrin + NADP(+)` (EC 1.14.13.43) | **RHEA:86147** `questin hydroquinone + NADP(+) = questin + NADPH + 2 H(+)` (EC **1.1.1.443**) — reductase step
**RHEA:86143** `questin hydroquinone + O2 = demethylsulochrin + 2 H(+)` (EC **1.13.11.96**) — dioxygenase / ring-opening step | The corresponding EC entry confirms the split: ``` ID 1.14.13.43 DE Transferred entry: 1.1.1.443 and 1.13.11.96. ``` ### Impact on go-edit.obo Only one term references RHEA:10836: - **GO:0050246** `questin monooxygenase (NADPH) activity` — has both a def-xref `[RHEA:10836]` and `xref: RHEA:10836 {source="skos:exactMatch"}`, plus `xref: EC:1.14.13.43 {source="skos:exactMatch"}` (also obsolete/transferred). MetaCyc and KEGG_REACTION xrefs remain. This is the only obsolete Rhea ID currently used anywhere in go-edit.obo (checked all xref and def-xref occurrences against the full Rhea obsolete list — 1184 obsolete reactions; only RHEA:10836 intersects with GO usage). No annotations need to be touched here (GO:0050246 itself is not obsoleted, just its mapping). The build failure in #32203 comes from `src/util/filter-rhea-xrefs.sc`, which exits 1 when any xref points to a Rhea-obsolete reaction. ### Options **A. Minimal — strip obsolete xrefs, keep term as-is.** Remove `xref: RHEA:10836` and `xref: EC:1.14.13.43` (and the corresponding def-xref provenance, replaced with e.g. `GOC:dragon-ai-agent`). Term stays with its current label/def. Unblocks #32203 immediately. Downside: leaves a term whose chemistry is no longer how Rhea/EC view this activity, with no new mappings. **B. Editorial — obsolete GO:0050246, mint two replacement terms.** *(recommended)* - Obsolete GO:0050246 with `consider:` (or `replaced_by:` if appropriate) pointing to the two new terms. - Create a new MF term for the reductase step → maps to RHEA:86147 / EC 1.1.1.443. - Create a new MF term for the questin-hydroquinone dioxygenase / anthraquinone-ring-opening step → maps to RHEA:86143 / EC 1.13.11.96. - Cite PMID:34586791. B faithfully captures the bienzyme mechanism Rhea/EC are now using and is consistent with how #31423 was handled for a simpler rename. The catch: this is a fungal secondary-metabolism activity (questin → demethylsulochrin in the emodin/sulochrin biosynthesis pathway), unlikely to have many existing annotations, but worth a curator's call on whether the obsoletion is warranted vs. a "redefine and remap" approach. ### Proposed next step Happy to proceed with either option. My recommendation is **B**, but it's a chemistry/curator call (cc @pgaudet @sjm41 who handled the analogous #31423). If consensus is "just unblock the build for now", I can ship A and leave a follow-up issue for the proper split. --- 🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent** - Model: `claude-opus-4-7` - Agent harness: claude-code - Triggered by: @balhoff - Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27623490394) ","createdAt":"2026-06-16T14:20:40Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32207#issuecomment-4719777828","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGVNvKA","author":{"login":"balhoff"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent please make a pull request implementing option B.","createdAt":"2026-06-16T14:29:15Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[{"content":"EYES","users":{"totalCount":1}}],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32207#issuecomment-4719865640","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGVOJ5Q","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"🤖 Working on it... Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27624917953) *— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-06-16T14:29:41Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32207#issuecomment-4719872485","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABGVaQGg","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Opened #32211 implementing option B. Summary of what's in the PR: - **Obsoleted GO:0050246** (`questin monooxygenase (NADPH) activity`) — synonyms preserved, `consider:` set to the two replacements, all `is_a`/xref tags stripped, def-xref switched from the obsolete `[RHEA:10836]` to `[PMID:34586791]`, comment explains the rationale. - **GO:7770080** `questin reductase (NADPH) activity` — new, parent `GO:0016616`, xrefs `EC:1.1.1.443` and `RHEA:86147`. - **GO:7770081** `questin hydroquinone dioxygenase activity` — new, parent `GO:0016702`, xrefs `EC:1.13.11.96` and `RHEA:86143`. All 16 SPARQL QC checks pass, ELK reasoning succeeds with both new terms classifying cleanly under their asserted parents, and `make go-edit-filtered-xrefs.ofn` (the script from #32203) now completes without any `Obsolete Rhea ID used` errors — so this should unblock the periodic Rhea refresh PR. Two existing annotations to GO:0050246 (`UniProtKB:Q0CCX5` gedK and `UniProtKB:Q4WQY9` tpcI, both *Aspergillus*) will need curator follow-up to migrate to one or both replacement terms — flagged in the PR description, not in scope for this PR. cc @pgaudet @sjm41 if you want to weigh in on the term shapes before merge. --- 🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent** - Model: `claude-opus-4-7` - Agent harness: claude-code - Triggered by: @balhoff - Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27624917953)","createdAt":"2026-06-16T14:48:35Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32207#issuecomment-4720070682","viewerDidAuthor":true}],"labels":[{"id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxOTM4Njc5MDk4","name":"go2rhea","description":"Issues related to the mapping GO-Rhea","color":"006b75"},{"id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyMTg5MjAyMDcw","name":"xref & mappings","description":"","color":"2434c1"}],"state":"OPEN","title":"Obsolete Rhea ID used in xref: RHEA:10836"}

content.content:
{"body":"Implements **Option B** from #32207. Closes #32207 (and unblocks #32203). ## Summary RHEA:10836 (`questin + NADPH + O2 = demethylsulochrin + NADP+`, EC:1.14.13.43) was obsoleted upstream and split into a two-enzyme mechanism (Qi et al. 2021, JACS, PMID:34586791): | Old (obsolete) | New replacements | |---|---| | RHEA:10836 / EC:1.14.13.43 | **RHEA:86147** / EC:**1.1.1.443** — questin hydroquinone + NADP+ = questin + NADPH + 2 H+ (reductase step)
**RHEA:86143** / EC:**1.13.11.96** — questin hydroquinone + O2 = demethylsulochrin + 2 H+ (dioxygenase / anthraquinone-ring-opening step) | GO:0050246 is the only term in `go-edit.obo` that references the obsolete RHEA:10836. Obsoleting it and minting the two replacements removes the last obsolete-Rhea-xref violation flagged by `src/util/filter-rhea-xrefs.sc`, unblocking the periodic Rhea refresh PR (#32203). ## Changes **Obsoleted:** - `GO:0050246` — renamed to `obsolete questin monooxygenase (NADPH) activity`, def prefixed with `OBSOLETE.`, def-xref switched from `[RHEA:10836]` to `[PMID:34586791]` (the paper describing the new mechanism — keeps the def-xref valid while removing the obsolete-Rhea reference), all `is_a`/xref tags stripped per the obsoletion convention, synonyms preserved, `consider:` set to both replacement terms, `comment:` documents the rationale, term tracker item added for #32207. **New:** - `GO:7770080` `questin reductase (NADPH) activity` — `is_a: GO:0016616`, xref `EC:1.1.1.443 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, xref `RHEA:86147 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, def from RHEA:86147. - `GO:7770081` `questin hydroquinone dioxygenase activity` — `is_a: GO:0016702`, xref `EC:1.13.11.96 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, xref `RHEA:86143 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, def from RHEA:86143, synonym `anthraquinone-ring-opening dioxygenase activity`. Both new terms cite PMID:34586791 in def-xref alongside the RHEA id. ## Annotation impact `runoak -i amigo: associations GO:0050246` returns 2 annotations: - `UniProtKB:Q0CCX5` (gedK), PMID:3182756, EXP - `UniProtKB:Q4WQY9` (tpcI), GO_REF:0000024, ISS Both are *Aspergillus* fungal secondary-metabolism gene products in the emodin/sulochrin biosynthesis pathway. These annotations will need to be remapped by curators after this PR is released — likely most should move to `GO:7770080` (the reductase, since the NADPH-dependent step is what the older `questin monooxygenase` label most directly tracked) or split across both replacements depending on the experimental evidence. Flagging for curator attention; not in scope for this PR. cc @pgaudet @sjm41. ## Checklist - [x] PLAN: issue analyzed, intent (option B) clear - [x] PRE-VALIDATION: ontology parsed cleanly before edits - [x] RESEARCH: prior investigation in #32207 (PMID:34586791, EC transferred entry 1.14.13.43 → 1.1.1.443 + 1.13.11.96) re-verified against current Rhea data - [x] TERM-SEARCH: confirmed GO:0050246 is the only term referencing RHEA:10836; parent candidates `GO:0016616` and `GO:0016702` verified via `obo-grep.pl` - [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS: naming/def patterns checked against prior art (`aldose reductase (NADPH) activity` GO:0004032 for the reductase; `3-hydroxyanthranilate 3,4-dioxygenase activity` GO:0000334 for the dioxygenase) - [x] EDITS: standard checkout / batch file / checkin procedure used - [x] RELATIONSHIPS: weak axiomatization (`is_a` to the EC-family-level parent), no `intersection_of` — consistent with sibling enzyme-activity terms; reasoner places both new terms cleanly - [x] SPECIALIZED-EDITS: `/term-obsoletion` (split with `consider:`, synonyms preserved, no logical axioms left, term tracker item added) and `/reaction` (RHEA + EC xrefs with `skos:exactMatch` qualifier, def from Rhea equation) skills consulted - [x] METADATA: `created_by: dragon-ai-agent` and `creation_date` on the two new terms only; existing GO:0050246 metadata not touched except for the obsoletion fields; `term_tracker_item` link to #32207 on all three terms - [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION: - `robot convert -i go-edit.obo -f obo` — parses - `robot verify` over all 16 SPARQL QC rules — all PASS, 0 violations - `robot reason -r ELK -i go-edit.obo` — succeeds; both new terms classify under their asserted parents - `make go-edit-filtered-xrefs.ofn` (the script from #32203) — completes successfully; no `Obsolete Rhea ID used in xref` errors - [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION: PMID:34586791 verified — Qi et al. (2021) *J Am Chem Soc* 143(40):16461–16468, \"Bienzyme-Catalytic and Dioxygenation-Mediated Anthraquinone Ring Opening\" — the paper that established the two-step mechanism Rhea now uses - [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED on the only edited file (`src/ontology/go-edit.obo`) - [N/A] CHEBI / `/chemical-entity`: no new ChEBI references added beyond what's implicit in the RHEA xrefs - [N/A] `/taxon-constraint`: no taxon constraint changes --- 🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent** - Model: \`claude-opus-4-7\` - Agent harness: claude-code - Triggered by: @balhoff - Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27624917953)","files":[{"path":"src/ontology/go-edit.obo","additions":33,"deletions":7}],"headRefName":"dragon-ai-agent-issue-32207-run8885","state":"OPEN","title":"Obsolete GO:0050246 (questin monooxygenase) — split into reductase + dioxygenase per Rhea (#32207)"}

{"body":"Implements **Option B** from #32207. Closes #32207 (and unblocks #32203). ## Summary RHEA:10836 (`questin + NADPH + O2 = demethylsulochrin + NADP+`, EC:1.14.13.43) was obsoleted upstream and split into a two-enzyme mechanism (Qi et al. 2021, JACS, PMID:34586791): | Old (obsolete) | New replacements | |---|---| | RHEA:10836 / EC:1.14.13.43 | **RHEA:86147** / EC:**1.1.1.443** — questin hydroquinone + NADP+ = questin + NADPH + 2 H+ (reductase step)
**RHEA:86143** / EC:**1.13.11.96** — questin hydroquinone + O2 = demethylsulochrin + 2 H+ (dioxygenase / anthraquinone-ring-opening step) | GO:0050246 is the only term in `go-edit.obo` that references the obsolete RHEA:10836. Obsoleting it and minting the two replacements removes the last obsolete-Rhea-xref violation flagged by `src/util/filter-rhea-xrefs.sc`, unblocking the periodic Rhea refresh PR (#32203). ## Changes **Obsoleted:** - `GO:0050246` — renamed to `obsolete questin monooxygenase (NADPH) activity`, def prefixed with `OBSOLETE.`, def-xref switched from `[RHEA:10836]` to `[PMID:34586791]` (the paper describing the new mechanism — keeps the def-xref valid while removing the obsolete-Rhea reference), all `is_a`/xref tags stripped per the obsoletion convention, synonyms preserved, `consider:` set to both replacement terms, `comment:` documents the rationale, term tracker item added for #32207. **New:** - `GO:7770080` `questin reductase (NADPH) activity` — `is_a: GO:0016616`, xref `EC:1.1.1.443 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, xref `RHEA:86147 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, def from RHEA:86147. - `GO:7770081` `questin hydroquinone dioxygenase activity` — `is_a: GO:0016702`, xref `EC:1.13.11.96 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, xref `RHEA:86143 {source=\"skos:exactMatch\"}`, def from RHEA:86143, synonym `anthraquinone-ring-opening dioxygenase activity`. Both new terms cite PMID:34586791 in def-xref alongside the RHEA id. ## Annotation impact `runoak -i amigo: associations GO:0050246` returns 2 annotations: - `UniProtKB:Q0CCX5` (gedK), PMID:3182756, EXP - `UniProtKB:Q4WQY9` (tpcI), GO_REF:0000024, ISS Both are *Aspergillus* fungal secondary-metabolism gene products in the emodin/sulochrin biosynthesis pathway. These annotations will need to be remapped by curators after this PR is released — likely most should move to `GO:7770080` (the reductase, since the NADPH-dependent step is what the older `questin monooxygenase` label most directly tracked) or split across both replacements depending on the experimental evidence. Flagging for curator attention; not in scope for this PR. cc @pgaudet @sjm41. ## Checklist - [x] PLAN: issue analyzed, intent (option B) clear - [x] PRE-VALIDATION: ontology parsed cleanly before edits - [x] RESEARCH: prior investigation in #32207 (PMID:34586791, EC transferred entry 1.14.13.43 → 1.1.1.443 + 1.13.11.96) re-verified against current Rhea data - [x] TERM-SEARCH: confirmed GO:0050246 is the only term referencing RHEA:10836; parent candidates `GO:0016616` and `GO:0016702` verified via `obo-grep.pl` - [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS: naming/def patterns checked against prior art (`aldose reductase (NADPH) activity` GO:0004032 for the reductase; `3-hydroxyanthranilate 3,4-dioxygenase activity` GO:0000334 for the dioxygenase) - [x] EDITS: standard checkout / batch file / checkin procedure used - [x] RELATIONSHIPS: weak axiomatization (`is_a` to the EC-family-level parent), no `intersection_of` — consistent with sibling enzyme-activity terms; reasoner places both new terms cleanly - [x] SPECIALIZED-EDITS: `/term-obsoletion` (split with `consider:`, synonyms preserved, no logical axioms left, term tracker item added) and `/reaction` (RHEA + EC xrefs with `skos:exactMatch` qualifier, def from Rhea equation) skills consulted - [x] METADATA: `created_by: dragon-ai-agent` and `creation_date` on the two new terms only; existing GO:0050246 metadata not touched except for the obsoletion fields; `term_tracker_item` link to #32207 on all three terms - [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION: - `robot convert -i go-edit.obo -f obo` — parses - `robot verify` over all 16 SPARQL QC rules — all PASS, 0 violations - `robot reason -r ELK -i go-edit.obo` — succeeds; both new terms classify under their asserted parents - `make go-edit-filtered-xrefs.ofn` (the script from #32203) — completes successfully; no `Obsolete Rhea ID used in xref` errors - [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION: PMID:34586791 verified — Qi et al. (2021) *J Am Chem Soc* 143(40):16461–16468, \"Bienzyme-Catalytic and Dioxygenation-Mediated Anthraquinone Ring Opening\" — the paper that established the two-step mechanism Rhea now uses - [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED on the only edited file (`src/ontology/go-edit.obo`) - [N/A] CHEBI / `/chemical-entity`: no new ChEBI references added beyond what's implicit in the RHEA xrefs - [N/A] `/taxon-constraint`: no taxon constraint changes --- 🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent** - Model: \`claude-opus-4-7\` - Agent harness: claude-code - Triggered by: @balhoff - Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27624917953)","files":[{"path":"src/ontology/go-edit.obo","additions":33,"deletions":7}],"headRefName":"dragon-ai-agent-issue-32207-run8885","state":"OPEN","title":"Obsolete GO:0050246 (questin monooxygenase) — split into reductase + dioxygenase per Rhea (#32207)"}

74-    ontrdf(axiom, OIO_HAS_DB_XREF, ref).
75-
76:error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " has an xref to an obsolete EC: ", ec)) :- ec_xref(term, ec), valid_term(term), obsolete_ec(ec).
77-error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " has an xref to an unknown EC: ", ec)) :- ec_xref(term, ec), valid_term(term), !ec(ec, _).
78:error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " has a definition xref to an obsolete EC: ", ec)) :- ec_defxref(term, ec), valid_term(term), obsolete_ec(ec).
79-error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " has a definition xref to an unknown EC: ", ec)) :- ec_defxref(term, ec), valid_term(term), !ec(ec, _).
80:error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " has a synonym xref to an obsolete EC: ", ec)) :- synonym_xref(term, _, ec), valid_term(term), obsolete_ec(ec).
81-error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " references an undeclared subset: ", subset)) :- subset_assertion(term, subset), !declared_subset(subset).
82-error(cat("ERROR: empty literal value for ", s, " ", p)) :- triple_with_literal(s, p, o), match("\"\".*", o).
83-error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " has a comment that seems to be a URI")) :- triple_with_literal(term, RDFS_COMMENT, o), match("\"http.*", o).
84-
85-.output error(filename="datalog-violations.tsv")

23-.decl term(iri: symbol)
24-.decl obsolete_term(iri: symbol)
25:.decl valid_term(iri: symbol)
26-.decl synonym_property(iri: symbol)
27-.decl declared_subset(iri: symbol)
28-.decl subset_assertion(term: symbol, subset: symbol)
29-.decl triple_with_literal(s: symbol, p: symbol, o: symbol)
30-.decl xref(term: symbol, ref: symbol)
--
52-obsolete_term(iri) :- term(iri), ontrdf(iri, OWL_DEPRECATED, "\"true\"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#boolean>").
53-
54:valid_term(iri) :- term(iri), !obsolete_term(iri).
55-
56-declared_subset(iri) :- ontrdf(iri, RDF_TYPE, OWL_ANNOTATION_PROPERTY), ontrdf(iri, RDFS_SUBPROPERTY_OF, OIO_SUBSET_PROPERTY).
57-subset_assertion(term, subset) :- ontrdf(term, OIO_IN_SUBSET, subset).
58-
59-xref(term, ref) :- ontrdf(term, OIO_HAS_DB_XREF, ref), term(term).
--
74-    ontrdf(axiom, OIO_HAS_DB_XREF, ref).
75-
76:error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " has an xref to an obsolete EC: ", ec)) :- ec_xref(term, ec), valid_term(term), obsolete_ec(ec).
77:error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " has an xref to an unknown EC: ", ec)) :- ec_xref(term, ec), valid_term(term), !ec(ec, _).
78:error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " has a definition xref to an obsolete EC: ", ec)) :- ec_defxref(term, ec), valid_term(term), obsolete_ec(ec).
79:error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " has a definition xref to an unknown EC: ", ec)) :- ec_defxref(term, ec), valid_term(term), !ec(ec, _).
80:error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " has a synonym xref to an obsolete EC: ", ec)) :- synonym_xref(term, _, ec), valid_term(term), obsolete_ec(ec).
81-error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " references an undeclared subset: ", subset)) :- subset_assertion(term, subset), !declared_subset(subset).
82-error(cat("ERROR: empty literal value for ", s, " ", p)) :- triple_with_literal(s, p, o), match("\"\".*", o).
83-error(cat("ERROR: ", term, " has a comment that seems to be a URI")) :- triple_with_literal(term, RDFS_COMMENT, o), match("\"http.*", o).
84-
85-.output error(filename="datalog-violations.tsv")

content.text: The QC rule predicates errors on `valid_term(term)`, which excludes obsoleted terms. PR #32211 obsoletes GO:0050246, so both the EC xref error and the synonym xref error vanish — same fix kills both #32207 and #32212. I'll post the analysis on #32212.

input.command:
gh issue comment 32212 --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Investigation

Yes — direct interaction with #32207. Both errors come from the same upstream change and the same GO term, and the fix already in PR #32211 (which addresses #32207) resolves both errors here as well.

### Why the two issues are one fix

EC:1.14.13.43 and RHEA:10836 were obsoleted in lockstep upstream — the EC entry reads `Transferred entry: 1.1.1.443 and 1.13.11.96` and Rhea split RHEA:10836 into RHEA:86147 + RHEA:86143. GO:0050246 (`questin monooxygenase (NADPH) activity`) is the only GO term carrying either of those obsolete IDs.

PR #32211 obsoletes GO:0050246 and mints two replacements (GO:7770080 reductase, GO:7770081 dioxygenase) carrying the new EC and RHEA IDs. As part of that obsoletion the diff strips both:

```
-xref: EC:1.14.13.43 {source="skos:exactMatch"}
-xref: RHEA:10836 {source="skos:exactMatch"}
```

### Both errors silenced — including the synonym-xref one

The QC rule at `src/util/ontology-qc.dl:80`:

```
error("... has a synonym xref to an obsolete EC: ...") :- synonym_xref(term, _, ec), valid_term(term), obsolete_ec(ec).
```

is guarded by `valid_term`, which is defined (line 54) as `term(iri), !obsolete_term(iri)`. Once PR #32211 sets `is_obsolete: true` on GO:0050246, it drops out of `valid_term` and the rule no longer fires — so the two surviving `synonym ... RELATED [EC:1.14.13.43]` lines on the obsoleted term (kept intentionally, since they document what the synonyms came from) do **not** re-trigger this error. Same goes for line 76 (the plain xref error), which is already moot once the `xref: EC:1.14.13.43` line is removed.

### Recommendation

No separate PR or follow-on commit needed. #32211 already lands the fix for both #32207 and #32212. Suggest:

- Hold this issue open as a tracker, close it on merge of #32211 (or mark it a duplicate of #32207).
- After #32211 merges, the periodic `refresh-obsolete-ec` PR (#32210) should be able to land cleanly — same way the periodic Rhea refresh (#32203) will.

If you'd prefer I amend #32211 to explicitly mention #32212 in the description (so the merge auto-closes both), happy to do that.

---
🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**
- Model: \`claude-opus-4-7\`
- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @balhoff
- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/27636312923)
EOF
)"

content.text:
Posted the analysis on #32212. Summary:

- **#32212 and #32207 are the same fix.** EC:1.14.13.43 and RHEA:10836 were obsoleted in lockstep upstream (EC `Transferred entry: 1.1.1.443 and 1.13.11.96`; RHEA split into 86147 + 86143), both on the same single GO term GO:0050246.
- **PR #32211 (already open) resolves both.** Its diff strips both `xref: EC:1.14.13.43` and `xref: RHEA:10836`, and obsoleting the term takes it out of `valid_term` in `src/util/ontology-qc.dl`, so the surviving `synonym ... [EC:1.14.13.43]` lines don't re-trigger the synonym-xref error either.
- **Recommendation:** no separate fix; close #32212 as a duplicate (or on merge of #32211). Offered to amend the PR description to auto-close both if preferred.
